english_meaning: to end up; in the end; finally; after all ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 to end up; in the end; finally; after all ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (た form) あげく（に） Noun + の
example_1:
2時間も待たされたあげく、結局に試合は延期になった。
2じかんもまたされたあげく、けっきょくにしあいはえんきになった。
2 jikan mo matasareta ageku, keekyoku ni shiai wa enki ni natta.
example_2:
毎日の残業のあげく、彼女は倒れて入院することになりました。
mainichi no zangyou no ageku, kanojo wa taorete nyuuin shita koto ni nari mashita.
After working overtime everyday, she collapsed and was hospitalized.
example_3:
人気のラーメン店に２時間も並んだあげく、売り切れで食べられなかった。
ninki no raamen mise ni 2 jikan mo naranda ageku, urikire de taberarena katta.
After waiting at the popular ramen shop for 2 hours, they ended up sold out and I couldn't eat anything.
example_4:
どの大学に入ろうかと、さんざん悩んだあげく、京都大学に決めた。
dono daigaku ni hairou ka to, sanzan nayanda ageku, kyouto daigaku ni kimeta.
After thinking it over carefully about which university to enter, I decided on Kyoto University.
example_5:
彼らは夫婦げんかを繰り返したあげく、とうとう離婚した。
karera wa fuufu genka o kurikaeshita ageku, toutou rikon shita.
After fighting countless times, they finally got a divorce.

english_meaning: or; either; maybe; perhaps; possibly ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 or; either; maybe; perhaps; possibly ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs , Japanese conjunctions
How to use 使い方 あるいは other option
example_1:
今日中にファックス、あるいは、メールで送ってください。
kyoujuu ni fakkusu, aruiwa meeru de okutte kudasai.
Please send it today via fax or mail.
example_2:
ご注文は電話か、あるいはインターネットでお願いします。
gochuumon wa, aruiwa intaanetto de onegai shimasu.
Please order either by phone or online.
example_3:
来週の火曜日の午後はどうですか。あるいは水曜日の午前でも構いませんが．．．
raishuu no kayoubi no gogo wa dou desuka. aruiwa suiyoubi no gozen demo kamai masen ga…
How about Tuesday afternoon next week? Or Wednesday morning also works.
example_4:
進学するか、あるいは就職するか私は迷っています。
shingaku suru ka, aruiwa shuushoku suru ka watashi wa mayotte imaus.
I'm not sure whether I should advance my education or start working.
example_5:
あるいはそれは本当かもしれない。
aruiwa sore wa hontou kamoshirenai.
Perhaps that's true.

english_meaning: about, approximately ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 about, approximately ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese particles , ばかり expressions
How to use 使い方 Noun (indicates time or distance) ばかり
example_1:
彼女は30分ばかりベッドに横になった。
kanojo wa 30 pun bakari beddo ni yoko ni natta.
She lay in bed for about a half-hour.
example_2:
５分ばかりこの道を行けば、右手にその店があります。
go fun bakari kono michi o ikeba, migite ni sono mise ga arimasu.
If you continue on this road for about 5 minutes, that shop will be on your right.
example_3:
さくら大学まで２時間ばかりで着くと思います。
sakura daigaku made 2 jikan bakari de tsuku to omoimasu.
I think it takes about 2 hours to get to Sakura University.
example_4:
今日は8時間ばかりプログラミングをやって、目が疲れた。
kyou wa 8 jikan bakari puroguramingu o yatte, me ga tsukareta.
Today I did programming for about 8 hours and my eyes are really tired.
example_5:
昨日は4時間ばかりハイキングをしたから、今日は筋肉が痛い。
kinou wa 4 jikan bakari haikingu o shita kara, kyou wa kinniku ga itai.
Because I went hiking yesterday for about 4 hours, today my muscles are sore.

english_meaning: simply because; on account of~ (negative result)
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 simply because; on account of~ (negative result)
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs , ばかり expressions
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual) ばかりに Noun (+ である) な-adjective + な/である い-adjective
example_1:
お金がないばかりに、今度の旅行に行けなかった。
okane ga nai bakari ni, kondo no ryokou ni ikenakatta.
I wasn't able to go on this trip since I don't have any money.
example_2:
ホラー映画を見たばかりに、怖くてなかなか寝られない。
horaa eiga o mita bakari ni, kowakute nakanaka nerarenai.
I watched a horror movie, which was really scary and now I can't seem to fall asleep.
example_3:
先輩のアドバイスを聞き入れなかったばかりに大きな失敗をしてしまった。
senpai no adobaisu o kiki irenakatta bakari ni ooki na shippai o shite shimatta.
I didn't listen to the advice of my senior and ended up making a huge mistake.
example_4:
彼の一言を信じたばかりにひどい目にあった。
kare no hitokoto o shinjita bakari ni hidoime ni atta.
I had a horrible experience all because I believed what he said.
example_5:
経験がないばかりに苦労した。
keiken ga nai bakari ni kurou shita.
I suffered since I lacked any experience.

english_meaning: by the way; in this connection; incidentally; (conjunction)
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 by the way; in this connection; incidentally; (conjunction)
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese conjunctions
How to use 使い方 ちなみに phrase
example_1:
これ、お土産だよ。ちなみにベトナムで買ったんだよ。
kore, omiyage da yo. chinamini betonamu de katta nda yo.
Here is a souvenir. I bought it in Vietnam by the way.
example_2:
ちなみに、私のことを覚えていますか？
chinamini, watashi no koto o oboete imasu ka?
By the way, do you remember me?
example_3:
ちなみに、私は刺身と寿司は好きではない。
chinamini, watashi wa sashimi to sushi wa suki dewa nai.
Incidentally, I do not like sashimi or sushi.
example_4:
N2に合格したよ！ちなみに満点だったよ！
N2 ni goukaku shita yo! chinamini manten datta yo!
I passed the N2! And by the way, I got a full score!
example_5:
ちなみに、私の日本語が間違ってたら遠慮なく言ってください。
chinamini, watashi no nihongo ga machigatte tarae enryo naku itte kudasai.
By the way, please feel free to tell me if my Japanese is wrong.

english_meaning: (not) at all; (not) in the least ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 (not) at all; (not) in the least ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～ない
How to use 使い方 ちっとも Verb (ない form)
example_1:
彼は酒はちっとも飲まない。
kare wa sake wa chitto mo nomanai.
He doesn't drink any alcohol at all.
example_2:
この商品はちっとも売れない。
kono shouhin wa chitto mo urenai.
This product does not sell at all.
example_3:
俺の妻は英語をちっとも知らない。彼女は日本語しか話せない。
ore no tsuma wa eigo o chitto mo shiranai. kanojo wa nihongo shika hanasenai.
My wife doesn't know any English. She only speaks Japanese.
example_4:
彼は父親の言うことをちっとも聞かない。
kare wa chichioya no iukoto o chitto mo kikanai.
He doesn't listen to anything his father says.
example_5:
彼は女の子の気持なんかちっともわからない。
kare wa onna no ko no kimochi nanka chitto mo wakarai.
He doesn't understand the feelings of young women one bit.

english_meaning: being the case; precisely because; as expected from ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 being the case; precisely because; as expected from ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual) だけあって だけのことはあって Noun な-adjective + な い-adjective
example_1:
このアパートは駅に近いだけあって、やっぱり家賃も高い。
kono apaato wa eki ni chikai dake atte, yappari yachin ga takai.
This apartment is close to the station, so as expected the rent is expensive.
example_2:
さすが大都会だけあって何かしら仕事がある。
sasuga daitokai dake atte nanika shira shigoto ga aru.
As expected of a big city, there is always work to do.
example_3:
料理教室に通っているだけあって、彼女の作る料理はとても美味しい。
ryouri kyoushitsu ni kayotte iru dake atte, kanojo no tsukuru ryouri wa totemo oishii.
She is taking lessons at a cooking school and so the food she makes is really delicious.
example_4:
このバッグは安いだけあってすぐに壊れてしまった。
kono baggu wa yasui dake atte sugu ni kowarete shimatta.
As expected of this cheap bag, it broke right away.
example_5:
この場所は有名なだけあって、たくさんの観光客がいる。
kono basho wa yuumei na dake atte, takusan no kankoukyaku ga iru.
This is a very famous place and so there are lots of tourists.

english_meaning: being the case; precisely because; as one would expect
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 being the case; precisely because; as one would expect
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual) だけに Noun な-adjective + な い-adjective
example_1:
駅が近いだけに家賃も高い。
eki ga chikai dake ni yachin mo takai.
The station is nearby, which is also why the rent is so high.
example_2:
このホテルは５つ星ホテルなだけに、サービスが充実している。
kono hoteru wa itsutsu boshi hoteru na dake ni, saabisu ga juujitsu shite iru.
As one would expect of a 5-star hotel, the service is perfect.
example_3:
彼女は日本語を20年教えているだけに、教え方がとても上手だ。
kanojo wa nihongo o 20 nen oshiete iru dake ni, oshiekata ga totemo jouzu da.
As expected of someone who has taught Japanese for 20 years, her teaching style is very good.
example_4:
田中さんは小学校の時にアメリカで生活していただけに、英語の発音はネイティブのようだ。
tanaka san wa shougakkou no toki ni amerika de seikatsu shiteita dake ni, eigo no hatsuon wa neitibu no you da.
Tanaka spent his elementary school days in America, and as one would expect his English pronunciation is just like a native.
example_5:
試験のために一生懸命勉強しただけに、不合格のショックは大きかった。
shiken no tame ni isshoukenmei benkyou shita dake ni, fugoukaku no shokku wa ookikatta.
I studied very hard for the exam, so I was really shocked when I heard that I failed.

english_meaning: to do all that one can
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 to do all that one can
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (dictionary form) だけは Same verb (past form)
example_1:
走れるだけは速く走った。
hashireru dake wa hayaku hashitta.
I ran as fast as my legs could carry me.
example_2:
私はあなたを手伝えるだけは手伝ったよ。
watashi wa anata o tetsudaeru dake wa tetsudatta yo.
I did everything I could to help you.
example_3:
彼氏のことは親に話すだけは話しました。
kareshi no koto wa oya ni hanasu dake wa hanashimashita.
I told my parents everything there is to say about my boyfriend.
example_4:
覚えるだけは覚えたのだから 、後は試験の日を待つだけだ。
oboeru dake wa oboeta no da kara, ato wa shiken no hi o matsu dake da.
I've memorized everything I need to know, so all that's left is to wait until the day of the test.
example_5:
すべて彼のことを聞くだけは聞いたが彼は悪い人を思わないよ。
subete kare no koto o kiku dake wa kiita ga kare wa warui hito o omowanai yo.
I heard everything I could about him, but I don't think he is a bad person.

english_meaning: because; but; after all; even; too
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 because; but; after all; even; too
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese conjunctions
How to use 使い方 Noun + だって even, too だって + phrase because, but
example_1:
彼だって人間だ。
kare datte ningen da.
He is only human.
example_2:
私だってあなたに会いたい。
watashi datte anata ni aitai.
I miss you too.
example_3:
誰だってそんなことは知っているさ。
dare datte sonna koto wa shitteiru sa.
Everybody knows that.
example_4:
私だって本くらい読むよ。
watashi datte hon kurai yomu yo.
Even I read books every once in a while.
example_5:
「何を怒ってるの？」「だって、約束を破ったじゃん。』
"Why are you upset?""Because you broke your promise!"

japanese_expression: どころではない(dokoro dewa nai)
english_meaning: not the time for; not the place for; far from; anything but ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 not the time for; not the place for; far from; anything but ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual) どころではない どころじゃない Noun
example_1:
彼はよく間違いをするが、バカどころではない。
kare wa yoku machigai o suru ga, baka dokoro dewa nai.
He often makes mistakes, but he is no fool.
example_2:
宿題がたくさんあってテレビを見るどころではない。
shukudai ga takusan atte terebi o miru dokoro dewa nai.
There's a bunch of homework to do, this is no time to be watching TV.
example_3:
風邪がひどくて、遊びに行くどころじゃない。
kaze ga hidokute, asobi ni iku doroko janai.
I have a bad cold and can't even think about going out right now.
example_4:
彼女はケーキ屋で働いているから、毎年クリスマスは彼氏とデートどころではない。
kanojo wa keekiya de hataraite iru kara, maitoshi kurisumasu wa kanojo to deeto dokoro dewa nai.
She works at a cake shop so every year on Christmas she has no time to go on a date with her boyfriend.
example_5:
他人の仕事を手伝うどころではありません。自分の仕事も間に合わないんです。
tanin no shigoto o tetsudau dokoro dewa arimasen. jibun no shigoto mo mani awanain desu.
There is no time to be helping other people with their work. You'll end up being late with your own work.

english_meaning: far from; anything but; let alone; not to mention; much less ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 far from; anything but; let alone; not to mention; much less ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～か , Japanese particles , ところ expressions
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual, non-past) どころか Noun な-adjective + (な) い-adjective
example_1:
もっと勉強しないと、N2どころかN3も無理だ。
motto benkyou shinaito, N2 dokoro ka N3 mo muri da.
If you do not study more, you will not be able to pass N2, let alone N3.
example_2:
そんな食べ物、健康になるどころか、病気になっちゃうよ。
sonna tabemono, kenkou ni naru dokoro ka, byouki ni nacchau yo.
That food isn't going to make you healthy, on the contrary it will make you sick.
example_3:
「夏休みは取れそう？」「忙しくて夏休みどころか日曜日も休めない。」
"Does it look like you can take summer break off?""I'm so busy I won't be able to take a summer break, much less even a Sunday off."
example_4:
医者に出された薬を飲んでいるのに、よくなるどころか、症状はひどくなる一方だよ。
isha ni dasareta kusuri o nondeiru noni, yoku naru dokoro ka, shoujou ga hidoku naru ippou da yo.
Even though I'm taking the medicine my doctor gave me, I am not getting better. On the contrary, my symptoms keep getting worse.
example_5:
私は彼に役に立つどころか、迷惑もかけた。
watashi wa kare ni yaku ni tatsu dokoro ka, meiwaku mo kaketa.
I was far from being of any help to him. Rather I got in his way.

english_meaning: possibly; apparently; seems like; somehow; barely ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 possibly; apparently; seems like; somehow; barely ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 どうやら phrase
example_1:
どうやら明日は雨になりそうだ。
dou yara ashita wa ame ni nari sou da.
Apparently, it's going to rain tomorrow.
example_2:
どうやら、彼は知らないらしい。
dou yara, kare wa shiranai rashii.
It seems he doesn’t know.
example_3:
どうやら誤解があるようだ。
dou yara gokai ga aru you da.
There appears to be a misunderstanding.
example_4:
今晩はどうやら雪が降りそうだ。
konban wa dou yara yuki ga furi sou da.
Apparently, it is going to snow tonight.
example_5:
どうやら日本の生活になれてきた。
dou yara nihon no seikatsu ni narete kita.
Somehow, I finally started getting used to life in Japan.

english_meaning: unable to; cannot; it is not possible to ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 unable to; cannot; it is not possible to ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: stem form grammar
How to use 使い方 Verb ます (stem form) 得ない Related Kanji 漢字 N3 Kanji: 得 (toku, eru) gain
example_1:
あの人が結婚したって本当！？え～！あり得ないよ！
ano hito ga kekkon shitatte hontou! ? e~! ari enai yo!
Is it true that he got married? No way! It's not possible!
example_2:
あの真面目な彼が犯人？そんなことはあり得ない。
ano majime na kare ga hannin? sonna koto wa ari enai.
You think someone as earnest as him is the culprit? That's unbelievable..
example_3:
地震がいつ来るかなんて、予測し得ないことだ。
jishin ga itsu kuru ka nante, yosoku shi enai kotoda.
It is unpredictable when an earthquake will occur.
example_4:
無から有は生じ得ない。
mu kara yuu wa shouji enai.
Nothing can come of nothing.
example_5:
機械は完全には人力に代わり得ない。
kikai wa kanzen ni wa jinriki ni kawari enai.
Machinery cannot completely take the place of human labor.

english_meaning: can; to be able to; is possible to ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 can; to be able to; is possible to ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb ます (stem form) 得る (える/うる) Related Kanji 漢字 N3 Kanji: 得 (toku, eru) gain
example_1:
君の話を信じるよ。だってこの世界ではどんなことでも起こり得るから。
kimi no hanashi o shinjiru yo. datte kono sekaide wa donna koto demo okori eru kara.
I believe in your story. I mean, anything is possible in this world.
example_2:
事故はいつでも起こり得るので、気をつけてください。
jiko wa itsu demo okori eru node, ki o tsukete kudasai.
Accidents can happen at any time, so please be careful!
example_3:
タバコを吸いすぎや、お酒を飲み過ぎは病気の原因になり得ます。
tabako o sui sugi ya, o sake o nomi sugi wa byouki no genin ni nari emasu.
Excessive smoking and drinking too much alcohol can cause illness.
example_4:
運動不足は病気の原因になり得るので、できるだけ体を動かすようにしてください。
undou fusoku wa byouki no genin ni nari eru node, dekiru dake karada o ugokasu youni shite kudasai.
Lack of exercise can cause illness, so try to move as much as you can.
example_5:
この不況では大手企業の倒産もあり得る。
kono fukyou dewa ootekigyou no tousan mo ariuru.
In this recession, it's possible some major companies may go bankrupt.

english_meaning: again; once more
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 again; once more
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 再び ふたたび phrase Related Kanji 漢字 N2 Kanji: 再 (sai, futatabi) again
example_1:
来週の月曜日からレッスンを再び開始したいですか？
raishuu no getsuyoubi kara ressun o futatabi kaishi shitai desu ka?
Would you like to restart your lessons from next Monday?
example_2:
今年の旅行は楽しかった。来年の冬再びここに来たい。
kotoshi no ryokou wa tanoshikatta. rainen no fuyu futatabi koko ni kitai.
This year's trip was fun. I want to come here again next winter.
example_3:
10年後にようやく彼は、再び故郷の町を見た。
10ねんごにようやくかれは、ふたたびこきょうのまちをみた。
10 nengo ni youyaku kare wa, futatabi kokyou no machi o mita.
example_4:
昨日私は、図書館で１ヶ月前に出会った少女と再び会った。
kinou watashi wa, toshokan de ikkagetsu mae ni deatta shoujo to futatabi atta.
Yesterday I once again met the girl whom I met in the library a month ago.
example_5:
本当に最悪の男だ。二度と再びあの男とは会いたくない。
hontou ni saiaku no otoko da. nido to futatabi ano otoko to wa aitakunai.
He really is the worst guy... I don't ever want to see him again.

english_meaning: this way; that way; in such a way; how

Meaning 意味 this way; that way; in such a way; how

vocabulary list

english_meaning: with… as a start; as a result of; taking advantage of ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 with… as a start; as a result of; taking advantage of ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (た form) + の/こと がきっかけで をきっかけに Noun

pasokon o katte moratta koto ga kikkake de, puroguramingu no benkyou o hajime mashita.
Taking advantage of the computer I received, I began studying programming.
example_5:
失恋をきっかけに、海外に出て仕事をしようと決心した。
shitsuren o kikkake ni, kaigai ni dete shigoto o shiyou to kesshin shita.
After experiencing heartbreak, I decided to move and work abroad.

On the other hand, what is a Japanese food that you hate?

example_1:
冷蔵庫に入れていたアイスクリームを食べたのは、娘に相違ない。
reizouko ni irete ita aisu kuriimu o tabeta nowa, musume ni souinai.
I'm certain it was my daughter who ate the ice cream I put in the freezer.
example_2:
その情報を漏らしたのは彼女に相違ない。
sono jouhou o morashita no wa kanojo ni soui nai.
The one who leaked that information must be her.
example_3:
この日本語学校は色々な国の学生がいる、楽しいに相違ない。
kono nihongo gakkou wa iroiro na kuni no gakusei ga iru, tanoshii ni souinai.
This Japanese language school has students from various countries, it will certainly be enjoyable.
example_4:
これだけの証拠があるなら、犯人はあの男に相違ない。
kore dake no shouko ga aru nara, hannin wa ano otoko ni souinai.
With this much evidence, without a doubt that man is the criminal.
example_5:
この地域の水汚染の問題を解決するのは難しいに相違ない。
kono chiiki no mizu osen no mondai o kaiketsu suru nowa muzukashii ni soui nai.
"Solving this area’s water pollution problem is certainly going to be difficult. "

example_1:

Meaning 意味 natural; as a matter of course

vocabulary list
related grammar

How to use 使い方 Verb (て form) 当然だ 当たり前だ な-adjective + で い-adjective + い くて Related Kanji 漢字 N3 Kanji: 当 (tou, ataru) hit N3 Kanji: 然 (zen, nen, shika, sa) Meaning: sort of thing, if so

mainichi osoku made zangyou shite iru kara, tsukarete touzen da.

example_2:

grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 not at all; there is not even one
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～ない
How to use 使い方 1 + [counter] + として + ... + Verb (ない form)
example_1:
僕は一日として学校を休んだことがない。
boku wa ichinichi toshite gakkou o yasunda koto ga nai.
I have never missed school for even a single day.
example_2:
彼女は一度として宿題を忘れたことがない。
kanojo wa ichido toshite shukudai o wasureta koto ga nai.
She has never once forgotten her homework.
example_3:
彼の提案に誰一人として反対できませんでした。
kare no teian ni dare hitori toshite hantai dekimasen deshita.
Not a single person could oppose his proposal.
example_4:
津波により家が流されてしまい、何１つとして残っていない。
tsunami ni yori ie ga nagasarete shimai, nan hitotsu toshite nokotte inai.
The house was washed away by the tsunami, and nothing remains.
example_5:
この人形は全て手作りなので、一つとして同じものがありません。
kono ningyou wa subete tedzukuri na node, hitotsu toshite onaji mono ga arimasen.
All of these dolls are handmade, so no one is the same as another.

english_meaning: assuming; even if ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 assuming; even if ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual form) としても Noun + だ な-adjective + だ い-adjective
example_1:
今回の試験で不合格になったとしても、諦めずに次回も受験するつもりです。
konkai no shiken de fugoukaku ni natta toshitemo, akiramezu ni jikai mo juken suru tsumori desu.
Even if I were to fail the exam this time, I plan to not give up and take the exam again.
example_2:
たとえ買えるとしても，そんな家は買わないだろう。
tatoe kaeru toshitemo, sonna ie wa kawanai darou.
I wouldn't buy such a house even if I could.
example_3:
学校を卒業後、国に帰ったとしても、日本語の勉強は続けるつもりです。
gakkou o sotsugyou go, kuni ni kaetta toshite mo, nihongo no benkyou wa tsuzukeru tsumori desu.
After I graduate school, even if I head to my home country, I plan to continue studying Japanese.
example_4:
私はお金持ちになったとしても、今と変わらない生活をするでしょう。
watashi wa okanemochi ni natta toshitemo, ima to kawaranai seikatsu o suru deshou.
Even if I were to become rich, my life likely wouldn't change much from what it is like now.
example_5:
彼を説得しようとしても無駄だ。
kare o settoku shiyou toshitemo muda da.
It's pointless to try to persuade him.

english_meaning: to be doing; to be in the process of doing ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 to be doing; to be in the process of doing ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: stem form grammar
How to use 使い方 Verb ます (stem form) つつある
example_1:
スマホの登場で、デジタルカメラの人気がなくなりつつある。
sumaho no toujou de, dejitaru kamera no ninki ga nakunari tsutsu aru.
Since the release of the smartphone, digital camera's have been losing popularity.
example_2:
最近はゴルフをしていないので、下手になりつつあります。
saikin wa gorufu o shite inai node, heta ni nari tsutsu arimasu.
Lately I haven't been playing golf, so I am losing my touch.
example_3:
日本は高齢化が進みつつある。
nihon wa koureika ga susumi tsutsu aru.
Japan's population continues to age.
example_4:
事故から一週間が経ち、原因などが明らかになりつつある。
jiko kara isshuukan ga tachi, genin nado ga akiraka ni nari tsutsu aru.
It's been one week since the accident and the cause of the accident is becoming clear.
example_5:
最近、買い物をしすぎてお金がなくなりつつあります。
saikin, kaimono o shisugite okane ga nakunari tsutsu arimasu.
Lately I've been shopping too much and am losing money.

english_meaning: anyhow; anyway; regardless; in any case; whether or not ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 anyhow; anyway; regardless; in any case; whether or not ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 Noun はともかく（として）
example_1:
行くか行かないかはともかく、レストランの予約だけはしておこう。
iku ka ikanai ka wa tomokaku, resutoran no yoyaku dake wa shite okou.
Whether or not we are going, let's at least make the restaurant reservation.
example_2:
この店の品物は、質はともかく種類は多い。
kono mise no shinamono wa, shitsu wa tomokaku shurui wa ooi.
Regardless of the quality of the goods at this store, they have a lot of variety.
example_3:
奥さんの料理は見た目はともかく味は美味しい。
okusan no ryouri wa mitame wa tomokaku aji wa oishii.
Regardless of weather it looks good or not, my wife's cooking tastes pretty good.
example_4:
漢字を読むことはともかく、書くことは苦手だ。
kanji o yomu koto wa tomokaku, kaku koto wa nigate da.
I can't even write kanji, let alone read it.
example_5:
あの俳優は、顔はともかくとして演技が素晴らしい。
ano haiyuu wa, kao wa tomokaku toshite engi ga subarashii.
Whether or not he/she has a nice face, they are a great actor.

english_meaning: slightly; only; barely; narrowly ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 slightly; only; barely; narrowly ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 わずかに Phrase
example_1:
電車がわずかに遅れている。
densha ga wazuka ni okurete iru.
The train is running slightly late.
example_2:
道はここでわずかに右に曲がっている。
michi wa koko de wazuka ni migi ni magatte iru.
The road bends slightly to the right here.
example_3:
財布の中に残っていたお金はわずかに500円だった。
saifu no naka ni nokotte ita okane wa 500 en datta.
The money remaining in the wallet was only 500 yen.
example_4:
私は学校へわずかに二ヶ月しか行っていません。
watashi wa gakkou e wazuka ni nikagetsu shika itte imasen.
I only went to school for barely 2 months.
example_5:
わずかに4日間でそんなにたくさんの仕事をしたとは驚きだ。
wazuka ni yokkakan de sonna ni takusan no shigoto o shita to wa odoroki da.
I'm surprised you did all that work in just four days.

english_meaning: before long; soon; almost; eventually ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 before long; soon; almost; eventually ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 やがて phrase
example_1:
やがて真っ暗になった。
yagate makkura ni natta.
It soon became completely dark.
example_2:
やがて考えがまとまってきた。
yagate kangae ga matomatte kita.
My ideas finally began to take shape.
example_3:
空は曇ってきた。やがて雨になるかもしれない。
sora wa kumotte kita. yagate ame ni naru kamo shirenai.
The sky is starting to get cloudy. It might start raining soon.
example_4:
やがてうちの近くの公園は桜が咲きます。
yagate uchi no chikaku no kouen wa sakura ga sakimasu.
Before long, the cherry blossoms will be blooming in the park near our house.
example_5:
彼が親と喧嘩して家を出てからやがて三年になる。
kare ga oya to kenka shite ie o dete kara yagate sannen ni naru.
It will soon be 3 years since he left home after fighting with his parents.

english_meaning: such things as A and B; A and B and so on ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 such things as A and B; A and B and so on ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese particles
How to use 使い方 Verb (dictionary) やら + [B]やら Noun い-adjective
example_1:
大学生は宿題やらアルバイトやらで忙しい。
daigakusei wa shukudai yara arubaito yara de isogashii.
University students are quite busy with things like homework and part time jobs.
example_2:
さっきコンビニでおにぎりやらお茶やらを買った。
sakki konbini de onigiri yara ocha yara katta.
A short while ago I bought some rice balls and tea from the convenience store.
example_3:
疲れやら空腹やらで、彼は死んだように倒れた。
tsukare yara kuufuku yara de, kare wa shinda you ni taoreta.
With fatigue and hunger, he fell down as if he were dead.
example_4:
サイクリングをする時は嬉しいやら楽しいやら、とてもいい気分だ。
saikuringu o suru toki wa ureshii yara tanoshii yara, totemo ii kibun da.
When I go cycling I feel very happy and have a lot of fun. It really is a great feeling!
example_5:
カバンにはパソコンやらノートやら教科書やらが入っている。
kaban ni wa pasokon yara nooto yara kyoukasho yara ga haitteiru.
In my backpack, I have things like my laptop, my notebook, textbook, etc..

japanese_expression: よほど / よっぽど(yohodo / yoppodo)
english_meaning: very; greatly; much; to a large extent
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 very; greatly; much; to a large extent
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: Japanese adverbs
How to use 使い方 よほど よっぽど explanation
example_1:
よほど前にあった事です。
yohodo mae ni atta koto desu.
It happened a long time ago.
example_2:
そこはここからよほど離れている。
soko wa koko kara yohodo hanareteiru.
That place is quite far from here.
example_3:
彼はこれまでよりよほどよく働いております。
kare wa kore made yori yohodo yoku hataraite orimasu.
He is working much harder than before.
example_4:
彼はよほどのことがなければ気持ちを変えないだろう。
kare wa yohodo no koto ga nakereba kimochi o kaenai darou.
It'll take a lot to make him change his mind.
example_5:
よほど本当のことを言ってやろうかと思った。
yohodo hontou no koto o itte yarou ka to omotta.
I had a good mind to tell him the truth.

japanese_expression: よりほかない(yori hoka nai)
english_meaning: to have no choice but ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 to have no choice but ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～ない
How to use 使い方 Verb (dictionary) （より）ほか（は）ない （より）ほか（しかたが）ない
example_1:
この仕事は君に信頼するよりほかはない。
kono shigoto wa kimi ni shinrai suru yori hoka wa nai.
I have no choice but to trust you with this job.
example_2:
バスがないので、歩いていくよりほか仕方がない。
basu ga nai node, aruite iku yori hoka shikata ga nai.
Since there are no buses, i have no choice but to go on foot.
example_3:
頼れる人はいないから、自分がやるよりほか仕方がない。
tayoreru hito wa inai kara, jibun ga yaru yori hoka shikata ga nai.
I have no one to depend on, so the only option is to do it myself.
example_4:
僕は学力が足りないから勉強で補うよりほかはない。
boku wa gakuryoku ga tarinai kara benkyou de oginau yori hoka wa nai.
I don't have great academic ability, so I need to make up for it by studying.
example_5:
自転車が壊れてしまった。学校へバスで行くよりほか仕方がない。
jitensha ga kowarete shimatta. gakkou e basu de iku yori hoka shikata ga nai.
My bicycle broke. Now I have no choice but to go to school by bus.

english_meaning: if ~ (bad result)
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 if ~ (bad result)
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: negative expressions , よう expressions
How to use 使い方 Verb (casual form) ようでは bad result
example_1:
この問題が分からないようではN２には合格できないよ。
kono mondai ga wakaranai you dewa N2 niwa goukaku dekinai yo.
If you don't understand this problem, then you won't be able to pass the N2.
example_2:
試験の前の日に勉強を始めるようでは、合格はできない。
shiken no mae no hi ni benkyou o hajimeru you dewa, goukaku wa dekinai.
If you start studying the day before the exam, you will not be able to pass.
example_3:
この問題が解決できないようでは困るよ。
kono mondai ga kaiketsu dekinai you dewa komaru yo.
If we can't resolve this problem, there's going to be trouble.
example_4:
最初の日から、仕事に遅刻するようでは悪い印象になるよ。
saisho no hi kara, shigoto ni chikoku suru you dewa warui inshou ni naru yo.
If you are late to work from the first day, it will leave a bad impression.
example_5:
報告書にこんなにミスがあるようでは、君に仕事を任せられない。
houkokusho ni konna ni misu ga aru you dewa, kimi ni shigoto o makase rarenai.
If there are this many errors in your written report, then I will not be able to entrust any work with you.

japanese_expression: ようか～まいか(you ka~mai ka)
english_meaning: whether or not; considering options
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 whether or not; considering options
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～か , よう expressions
How to use 使い方 V (volational) + か V + まいか Special rules for 2nd verb Ru-verb: dictionary form OR ます + まい U-verb: dictionary form + まい くる: こまい / くるまい する: しまい / すまい / するまい
example_1:
雨だ。買い物をしに行こうか、行くまいか。
ame da. kaimono o shi ni ikou ka, iku mai ka.
It's raining.. Should I go to buy groceries or not?
example_2:
本当のことを話そうか話すまいか迷ったが、結局全部話した。
hontou no koto o hanasou ka hanasu mai ka mayotta ga, kekkyoku zenbu hanashita.
I didn't know whether to say the truth or not, but in the end I told everything.
example_3:
彼と結婚しようかするまいか悩むくらいなら、やめたほうがいいと思う。
kare to kekkon shiyou ka suru mai ka nayamu kurai nara, yameta hou ga ii to omou.
If you're not sure if you want to marry him or not, I think it's best you don't.
example_4:
その新聞記者は、記事を掲載しようかすまいか悩んだあげく、掲載をやめた。
sono shinbun kisha wa, kiji o keisai shiyou ka su mai ka nayanda ageku, keisai o yameta.
That newspaper reporter was deciding whether or not to to submit an article for publication, but ended up worrying too much and decided not to.
example_5:
留学しようかすまいか迷うこと自体働かなければならない者から見れば幸せなことだと思う。
ryuugaku shiyouka sumaika mayou koto jitai hatarakanakereba naranai mono kara mireba shiawasena koto da to omou.
I'm unsure if I should study abroad or not, but for people who are unable to go because of work, it is surely a wonderful dream experience.

japanese_expression: ざるを得ない(zaru o enai)
english_meaning: cannot help (doing); have no choice but to ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 cannot help (doing); have no choice but to ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: ～ない
How to use 使い方 Verb (ない root) ない ざるを得ない Exception: しない -> せ Related Kanji 漢字 N3 Kanji: 得 (toku, eru) gain
example_1:
私はそうせざるを得ない。
watashi wa sou sezaru o enai.
I have no choice but to do that.
example_2:
いやな仕事でも、生活のためには続けざるを得ない。
iya na shigoto demo, seikatsu no tame niwa tsuduke eru o enai.
Even if it's work you don't like, you must continue working in order to maintain your livelihood.
example_3:
彼は受験のため、サークル活動を休まざるを得なかった。
kare wa juken no tame, saakuru katsudou o yasuma zaru o enakatta.
In order to prepare for exams, he had no choice but to quit his club activities.
example_4:
彼女は進学したいが、家族が学費を払える状況でもないので、諦めざるを得ない。
kanojo wa shingaku shitai ga, kazoku ga gakuhi o haraeru joukyou demo nai no de, akirame zaru o enai.
She wants to go to university, but her family is not in a position to help support her so she has no choice but to give up.
example_5:
今回の旅行を楽しみにしていたが、母が急に入院したので、行くのをキャンセルせざるを得ない。
konkai no ryokou o tanoshimi ni shite ita ga, haha ga kyuu ni nyuuin shita node, iku no o kyanseru sezaru o enai.
I was looking forward to this trip, but my mother was suddenly hospitalized so we had to cancel it.

english_meaning: get by without doing ~
grammar_structure:
Meaning 意味 get by without doing ~
example sentences
vocabulary list
related grammar
Level: JLPT N2
Tags: without , ない stem grammar
How to use 使い方 Verb ない (ない stem) ずに済む Exception: する => せずに済む Related Kanji 漢字 N3 Kanji: 済 (sai, sei, sumu) Meaning: settle, relieve, finish
example_1:
今年は涼しかったので、エアコンを使わずに済みました。
kotoshi wa suzushikatta node, eakon o tsukawazu ni sumimashita.
This year was cool and I got by without using my air conditioner.
example_2:
ここでは1日中雨が降らずに済むことはほとんどない。
koko de wa ichinichichuu ame ga furazu ni sumu koto wa hotondo nai.
Hardly a day passes here without rain.
example_3:
損をせずに済んだ。
son o sezu ni sunda.
I got off without any loss.
example_4:
私の友達が自分のアパートに泊めてくれたのでホテルに泊まらずに済んだ。
watashi no tomodachi ga jibun no apaato ni tomete kureta node hoteru ni tomarazu ni sunda.
My friend let me stay at their apartment, so I got by without having to stay at a hotel.
example_5:
死なずに済んだのはまったくの幸運だった。
shinazu ni sunda no wa mattaku no kouun datta.
I escaped death by pure luck.
